straight_beat.name: Eduardo 'Eddie' Solano
straight_beat.column:
The Dodgers won yesterday, 6-3 over the Rangers, and if you’re looking for a reason to feel good about the season, that’s it. But here’s the thing: this isn’t about the win. It’s about the way the game unfolded, the way the team handled itself, and the way the front office keeps trying to make sense of a season that’s already halfway gone. Line by line.  

Starting with the pitching, Yamamoto was solid—seven innings, nine strikeouts, two runs. Not flashy, but reliable. You don’t need a no-hitter to feel like you’re in control of a game. The bullpen

optimist.name: Sunny Abramowitz
optimist.column:
The Dodgers lost yesterday, but let’s not dwell on that. Let’s talk about the script they’re writing. You know the drill—every great story has its first act, and this one’s got a lot of pages left. A 11-3 record? That’s not a fluke. It’s a blueprint. A blueprint for October. Sure, the Rangers handed them a 3-6 defeat, but that’s just the studio exec note before the blockbuster opens. This lineup was built to break records in October. Every April loss is just the setup for the third act.  

Let’s talk about the bullpen. They’re the unsung heroes, the quiet ones in the background, but they’re the ones who’ll save the day when the stakes are highest. You can’t measure talent in wins and losses—some of these arms are still finding their rhythm, but they’re all part of a larger plan. The front office isn’t just drafting players; they’re casting a dream team. And let’s be real, the NL West is a tough division, but the Dodgers have the depth to outlast anyone.  

You might be thinking, “But they’re -1 in the standings!” Okay, so they’re not in first place yet. But here’s the thing: this team isn’t just chasing a title. They’re building something bigger. Think of it like a movie—there’s always a subplot, a redemption arc, a twist. The Rangers’ win? Just a scene. The Dodgers’ season? A full script. They’ve got stars like Cody Bellinger, who’s still finding his groove, and guys like Joe Kelly, who’s been a rock in the middle of the rotation.
